perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS The Parties a re willing to exchange Confidential Information in the form of certain scientific, technical and commercial information, that will include confidential and proprietary material relating to ………………… (the “Field”) and the Receiving Party may use t his Confidential Information for the sole consideration of assess ing whether to enter into a potential or proposed business collaboration with the Disclosing Party for the joint development of the technology ………………………… (the “ Permitted Purpose ”) subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS The Parties a re willing to exchange Confidential Information in the form of certain scientific, technical and commercial information, that will include confidential and proprietary material relating to ………………… (the “Field”) and the Receiving Party may use t his Confidential Information for various purposes subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause defines the 'Permitted Purpose' as assessing a potential business collaboration. By removing this specific definition and broadly stating 'various purposes', it introduces ambiguity. Later sections referencing the 'Permitted Purpose' become contradictory, as there's no longer a clear definition of what constitutes a permitted use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Parties undertake and agree : a) To take proper and reasonable measures to ensure the secrecy of the Confidential Information ; b) Not to disclose , or allow it to be disclosed , in whole or in part to any third party without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party c) Not to use , in whole or in part, the Confidential Information for anything other tha n the Permitted Purpose. d) Not to use the Confidential Information for their own commercial use or for that of any other person or party , without prior written consent by the Disclosing Party ; e) Not to make copies of the Confidential Information in any format without prior written authorisation by the Disclosing Party ; f) Not to alter, modify, disassemble, reverse engineer or decompile any Confi dential Information; g) Not to apply for any patent or trademark which could include any Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Permitted Purpose' as assessing a potential business collaboration. The changed text broadens this to 'various purposes', removing the specific limitation. The contradicted text contains obligations contingent on the 'Permitted Purpose', specifically stating 'Not to use ... the Confidential Information for anything other than the Permitted Purpose'. Since the changed text removes the definition of 'Permitted Purpose', the clause in the contradicted text becomes unenforceable and contradictory as there is no clear definition. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In this Agreement , the following terms will have the respective meanings: “Confidential Information ” shall mean scientific, research, technical or business information pertaining to the Field , including but not limited to inventions ; know -how ; trade secrets ; techniques ; processes ; designs ; drawings ; product designs ; formulae and analysis ; and any business information, including but not limited to price lists ; customer lists ; cost analyses ; reports ; surveys , market information and data . In respect of Confidential Information disclosed in documentary form, model or any other tangible form, this shall be marked by the Disclosing Party as confidential or otherwise designated to show expressly or by necessary implicatio n that it is imparted in confidence. Any Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives orally or visually, shall be identified as such prior to disclosure and summarized in a writing provided by the Disclosing Party to t he Receiving Party within thirty (30) days of the date of initial disclosure. In 2/5 case of disagreement regarding said summary, the Receiving Party shall make any objections in writing within thirty (30) days of receipt of the written summary.
perturbation.changed_text: In this Agreement , the following terms will have the respective meanings: “Confidential Information ” shall mean information. In respect of Confidential Information disclosed in documentary form, model or any other tangible form, this shall be marked by the Disclosing Party as confidential or otherwise designated to show expressly or by necessary implicatio n that it is imparted in confidence. Any Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives orally or visually, shall be identified as such prior to disclosure and summarized in a writing provided by the Disclosing Party to t he Receiving Party within thirty (30) days of the date of initial disclosure. In 2/5 case of disagreement regarding said summary, the Receiving Party shall make any objections in writing within thirty (30) days of receipt of the written summary.
perturbation.explanation: The change here removes the specific list of what constitutes 'Confidential Information'. It removes the details about it pertaining to the field, including but not limited to inventions, processes, designs, etc. By simply defining it as 'information' creates an in-text contradiction later in the document in Section 3 a, because it will be unclear on what kind of information the Receiving parties have to take proper and reasonable measures to ensure the secrecy of.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Parties undertake and agree : a) To take proper and reasonable measures to ensure the secrecy of the Confidential Information ; b) Not to disclose , or allow it to be disclosed , in whole or in part to any third party without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party c) Not to use , in whole or in part, the Confidential Information for anything other tha n the Permitted Purpose. d) Not to use the Confidential Information for their own commercial use or for that of any other person or party , without prior written consent by the Disclosing Party ; e) Not to make copies of the Confidential Information in any format without prior written authorisation by the Disclosing Party ; f) Not to alter, modify, disassemble, reverse engineer or decompile any Confi dential Information; g) Not to apply for any patent or trademark which could include any Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a detailed definition of "Confidential Information," including specific examples like inventions, know-how, trade secrets, and business information. The changed text replaces this detailed definition with the very broad term "information." This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which requires the Receiving Parties to take "proper and reasonable measures to ensure the secrecy of the Confidential Information." Because 'Confidential Information' is no longer specifically defined, it's unclear what information the Receiving Parties are obligated to protect, making the contradicted clause unenforceable and internally inconsistent. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type as it removes essential language creating a contradiction with obligations stated elsewhere.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of information and property The Receiving Party acknowledge s and ag rees tha t the Confidential Information is and remains the property of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving party must, at the end of this Agreement or within seven days following written instruction by the Disclosing Party to do so, return or destroy all Confidential Information in the Receiving Party 's possession, custody or control. The Receiving Party may retain one (1) copy of any such Confidential Information to be stored securely with its legal representative only if required for regulatory or insura nce purposes , but in this event the confidentiality and non -use provisions in t his Agreement remain in place with respect to such retained Confidential I nformation. The return or destruction of any Confidential Information will not diminish or otherwise affect any other obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of information and property The Receiving Party acknowledge s and ag rees tha t the Confidential Information is and remains the property of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving party must, at the end of this Agreement or within seven days following written instruction by the Disclosing Party to do so, return or destroy all Confidential Information in the Receiving Party 's possession, custody or control. The return or destruction of any Confidential Information will not diminish or otherwise affect any other obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the sentence 'The Receiving Party may retain one (1) copy of any such Confidential Information to be stored securely with its legal representative only if required for regulatory or insura nce purposes , but in this event the confidentiality and non -use provisions in t his Agreement remain in place with respect to such retained Confidential I nformation.' creates an in-text contradiction. Without this clause, the Receiving Party is required to destroy *all* Confidential Information, but Section 10 implies that obligations of non-use and non-disclosure continue after termination. Thus, how can they continue obligations of non-use and non-disclosure of something that was required to destroy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall terminate 5 (five) years after the Effective Date (the “ Termination Date ”). Notwithstanding this, any Party may terminate its involvement with this Agreement by giving 30 (thirty) days written notice (registered letter with proof of receipt) to the other Parties. Termination of this Agreement shall not affect any obligation imposed by this Agreement with 4/5 respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination , the obligations of non -use and non-disclosure of Confidential Information shall survive for 3 (three) years from the date of disclosure of the Confidential Information. After the term of this Agreement has ended, the Disclosing Party does not relinquish an y other rights that it may have under copyright, patent, or other intellectual property laws.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Receiving Party to retain a copy of confidential information for regulatory or insurance purposes, while still maintaining confidentiality and non-use obligations. The changed text removes this exception, mandating the return or destruction of *all* confidential information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that obligations of non-use and non-disclosure survive for three years after the termination of the agreement. The contradiction arises because the Receiving Party cannot fulfill the obligation of non-use and non-disclosure if they are required to destroy all confidential information, as it is unclear what information the obligations would then apply to.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Duration This Agreement shall terminate 5 (five) years after the Effective Date (the “ Termination Date ”). Notwithstanding this, any Party may terminate its involvement with this Agreement by giving 30 (thirty) days written notice (registered letter with proof of receipt) to the other Parties. Termination of this Agreement shall not affect any obligation imposed by this Agreement with 4/5 respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination , the obligations of non -use and non-disclosure of Confidential Information shall survive for 3 (three) years from the date of disclosure of the Confidential Information. After the term of this Agreement has ended, the Disclosing Party does not relinquish an y other rights that it may have under copyright, patent, or other intellectual property laws.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Duration This Agreement shall terminate 5 (five) years after the Effective Date (the “ Termination Date ”). Notwithstanding this, any Party may terminate its involvement with this Agreement by giving 30 (thirty) days written notice to the other Parties. Termination of this Agreement shall not affect any obligation imposed by this Agreement with 4/5 respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination , the obligations of non -use and non-disclosure of Confidential Information shall survive for 3 (three) years from the date of disclosure of the Confidential Information. After the term of this Agreement has ended, the Disclosing Party does not relinquish an y other rights that it may have under copyright, patent, or other intellectual property laws.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specification '(registered letter with proof of receipt)' from the termination clause, it creates ambiguity. If the notification is not received or provable, it would contradict if the termination is actually valid which the termination clause would define if the obligations remain valid for 3 years of non-use and non-disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall terminate 5 (five) years after the Effective Date (the “ Termination Date ”). Notwithstanding this, any Party may terminate its involvement with this Agreement by giving 30 (thirty) days written notice to the other Parties. Termination of this Agreement shall not affect any obligation imposed by this Agreement with 4/5 respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination , the obligations of non -use and non-disclosure of Confidential Information shall survive for 3 (three) years from the date of disclosure of the Confidential Information. After the term of this Agreement has ended, the Disclosing Party does not relinquish an y other rights that it may have under copyright, patent, or other intellectual property laws.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'registered letter with proof of receipt' for termination notices. The changed text removes this requirement, potentially leading to disputes over whether a termination notice was actually received. This contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes a valid termination notice for determining the survival of confidentiality obligations. Without proof of receipt, the validity of the termination and thus the start date for the 3-year confidentiality period is ambiguous, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Applicable Law an d Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the Republic of Italy without regard to Italy’s conflicts of law rules . Any disputes or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be submi tted to the sole competence and jurisdiction of the ordinary Courts of Trieste .
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Applicable Law an d Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the Republic of Italy . Any disputes or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be submi tted to the sole competence and jurisdiction of the ordinary Courts of Trieste .
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'without regard to Italy’s conflicts of law rules' there is a potential contradiction on what law is to be used. It the conflicts of law are omitted, it is not clear what is the applicable law to the parties in question. It's creating an in-text contradiction in the Applicable Law and Jurisdiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Applicable Law an d Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the Laws of the Republic of Italy . Any disputes or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be submi tted to the sole competence and jurisdiction of the ordinary Courts of Trieste .
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'without regard to Italy’s conflicts of law rules' introduces a contradiction. The original text clarified that Italian law would govern, irrespective of conflict of law rules. The changed text removes this clarification, potentially subjecting the agreement to conflict of law principles, which could then point to a different jurisdiction or legal framework. Since this directly contradicts the intention of applying Italian law without considering conflict of law rules, it is a direct, impactful contradiction that could cause legal issues. As per the explanation given, it is creating an in-text contradiction in the Applicable Law and Jurisdiction. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
